categories.label: Apps & Products
categories.description: User-facing apps, hosted products, and commercial references that help builders understand complete sports AI workflows.

categories.label: Open-Source Projects
categories.description: Runnable repositories and prototypes that implement a sports AI workflow end to end.

categories.label: Datasets/APIs/Feeds
categories.description: Public data, sports APIs, live feeds, and reference datasets for building sports AI products.

categories.label: Developer Libraries/SDKs
categories.description: Packages, SDKs, and infrastructure libraries that help developers parse, normalize, visualize, and process sports data.

categories.label: AI Models/Components
categories.description: Reusable AI components for computer vision, modeling, LLM/NLP, media, and training-load workflows.

categories.label: Research Benchmarks
categories.description: Papers, benchmark datasets, simulation environments, and reproducible research references.

categories.label: Event Toolkits
categories.description: Event-specific builder kits, live data contracts, generated visuals, and matchday workflows.

categories.label: Learning Collections
categories.description: Awesome lists, handbooks, tutorials, and curated reference collections for sports AI builders.

recipes.title: Build Your Own xG Model
recipes.goal: Train and document a basic expected-goals model from public soccer event data.

recipes.title: Build Your Own Coaching Video Tagger
recipes.goal: Create a simple sports video tagging and coach-note workflow from user-owned footage.

recipes.title: Build Your Own Player Scouting Board
recipes.goal: Combine player profiles, public data, similarity charts, clips, and reports into a lightweight scouting board.

recipes.title: Build Your Own Athlete Load Dashboard
recipes.goal: Parse athlete-owned workload files, calculate load ratios, flag risk, and render a training dashboard.

recipes.title: Build Your Own Match Intelligence Report
recipes.goal: Normalize fixture and event data into an automated match report with model outputs and stat cards.

recipes.title: Build Your Own Sports AI Prototype Launch Pack
recipes.goal: Start from runnable prototypes and event kits when you want a compact demo that can be forked, explained, and shared.

scenes.title: HYROX
scenes.tagline: Functional fitness race with running segments and standardized workout stations — race operations, station-by-station performance analysis, and movement-standard enforcement, mapped to reusable sports AI resources.

sections.title: Event Operations
sections.problem: A HYROX event needs heat management, athlete check-in, station flow, score updates, and venue communication without forcing volunteers to reconcile spreadsheets by hand.

starterBuild.input: Event roster CSV, division rules, heat windows, station capacity, volunteer assignments.
starterBuild.output: Heat schedule, station load forecast, conflict report, and operator checklist.

sections.title: Training Plan Generation
sections.problem: Coaches and athletes need science-based HYROX programming that balances running volume, station work, periodization, and race-division standards instead of generic fitness plans.

starterBuild.input: Athlete division, race date, fitness level, weekly availability, and equipment access.
starterBuild.output: Periodized daily HYROX plan with aerobic-volume guardrails, race-weight progression, and validation notes.

sections.title: Station Split Analysis
sections.problem: Coaches and athletes need to know whether time loss came from running segments, transition flow, or a specific workout station.

starterBuild.input: Athlete split table, station names, run-lap times, division baselines, optional heart-rate zones.
starterBuild.output: Station-loss chart, run-vs-station profile, pacing consistency score, and coach notes.

sections.title: Movement Standard Verification
sections.problem: Standardized movements such as wall balls, sled pushes, burpee broad jumps, and lunges need consistent review without replacing human judges.

starterBuild.input: Athlete-owned station video, movement standard checklist, camera-angle notes.
starterBuild.output: Annotated rep timeline, range-of-motion warnings, uncertainty notes, and judge-review export.

gaps.title: HYROX heat and station load planner
gaps.description: Roster plus venue constraints -> heat plan and staffing risk report.

gaps.title: HYROX movement standard checker
gaps.description: Athlete-owned station video -> annotated review timeline with explicit uncertainty.

gaps.title: HYROX venue readiness checklist
gaps.description: Station inventory plus floor plan -> setup gaps and volunteer task list.

lanes.title: Train
lanes.focus: Between races — where athletes spend most of the year, and where search volume lives.

lanes.title: Race
lanes.focus: Race weekend — short window, high emotion, shareable outputs.

lanes.title: Analyze
lanes.focus: After the gun — the highest-traffic questions in the sport.

lanes.title: Judge
lanes.focus: Movement standards — the sport's hardest, most defensible computer-vision problem.

lanes.title: Operate
lanes.focus: Race ops — fewer users, bigger wins across a season of weekends.

lanes.title: Find
lanes.focus: Discovery — where new athletes enter the sport.

lanes.title: Data
lanes.focus: The substrate — everything above sits on it.

buildTargets.description: Target finish time -> per-run and per-station split plan, adjusted by division and runner-vs-strength bias.
Constraint-aware session builder: compromised-running blocks, station EMOMs, race-week tapers.
Coding-agent skill that turns athlete constraints into periodized daily HYROX plans, backed by reference files and a validator.
Division sled loads normalized for floor surface and sled type, with community friction notes.
Merges run volume and strength tonnage into one weekly readiness view; pairs with AthleteLoadMonitor and ACWR.
SkiErg and RowErg 1000 m pacing tables with damper and drag-factor presets, printable.
Gear, fueling, and warmup timeline generator that prints to one page.
Printable wristband of target splits, generated from a pacing plan.
Venue map plus realistic roxzone transit budgets, so race plans survive contact with the floor.
Exported result table -> run/station deltas, rank movement, and coach-ready notes.
Percentile tables by division, age group, and station from public results.
Station improvement scenarios -> projected finish time and rank shift; a thin layer on the split parser.
Multi-race progress for one athlete: fitness trend, station deltas, PB tracker.
Athlete-owned station video -> annotated review timeline with explicit uncertainty.
Pose keypoints -> rep segmentation for wall balls, burpee broad jumps, and lunges.
Annotated open clip set of common no-reps per station, for athlete education and judge onboarding.
Roster plus venue constraints -> heat plan and staffing risk report.
Station inventory plus floor plan -> setup gaps and volunteer task list.
Volunteer and judge rota builder with station rotation and break rules.
Finds HYROXCN certified gyms from coordinates, region keywords, browser location, or imported JSON exports.
Season calendar aggregator with registration-window alerts.
Doubles and relay partner matching by target time, division, and city.
Normalized JSON schema for results, stations, and divisions — the zone's data contract.
Python client that parses public result pages into the schema; FastF1 is the role model.
Labeled station-clip dataset for pose and rep-counting models, with model cards.
